job_description:
At Sana Commerce, we're looking for a Data Engineer (ML/AI) to design, build, and scale data systems that power our analytics and machine learning initiatives. Your work will ensure high-quality, reliable, and ML-ready data pipelines that enable both traditional analytics and advanced AI-driven solutions across the business.

 

What you'll be doing

Designing and maintaining data pipelines optimized for ML/AI workloads, including handling of large-scale, unstructured, and semi-structured data.
Building feature pipelines and feature stores that ensure reusability and consistency of data used by machine learning models.
Collaborating with Data Scientists and ML Engineers to understand data requirements for training, validation, and production deployment.
Ensuring data quality, lineage, and governance meet standards required for AI/ML applications.
Supporting MLOps practices by integrating data pipelines with model training, monitoring, and deployment workflows.
Leveraging distributed processing frameworks (e.g., Spark, Databricks, Azure Synapse) for scalable ML data processing.
job_requirements:
What you bring

8+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, working with Azure and Databricks, ideally with exposure to ML/AI-related data workflows.
College degree that demonstrates your analytic abilities, such as Econometrics, Computer Sciences, Mathematics or similar;
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills;
Experience with data preparation for ML/AI: managing large datasets, feature engineering, and real-time or batch data pipelines.
Familiarity with MLOps concepts and how data engineering supports model lifecycle management.
Experience with orchestration frameworks (Airflow, Prefect, or Azure Data Factory) for complex ML pipelines.
Knowledge of unstructured data processing (text, images, logs) is a plus.
Strong SQL and Python skills; experience with distributed data processing (PySpark, Dask, etc.) is a plus.

Why you’ll love working here

Impact from day one – Join a scale-up where your ideas shape how global businesses operate online.
Continuous learning – Access a structured onboarding rated 9.1/10 by previous hires, mentorship, and feedback culture.
Hybrid flexibility – Work from our office 3 days per week and from home 2 days.
Career growth – Expand your technical and leadership scope in a company built for long-term success.

Our values

At Sana Commerce, our values drive everything we do:

Champions of Our League – We deliver lasting success, balancing quick wins and long-term value
Supercharge Our Customers – We’re revolutionizing B2B commerce together, helping our customers to lead and succeed.
Determined to Grow – We embrace challenges, growing and raising the bar for ourselves and our industry.

Ready to build reliability that scales?

Apply now and help shape the foundation of our next-generation SaaS platform.

job_description:
Ayra AI is a Canadian startup building the AI operating system for home service businesses. Not a chatbot. Not a simple answering service. A full operating layer — one that handles calls, books jobs, follows up with customers, and runs the front office so contractors can focus on the work only humans can do.
We're based in beautiful British Columbia, and we're obsessed with one thing: making world-class AI actually work in the real world. Home service businesses — HVAC, plumbing, roofing, pest control — are the backbone of every community, and they've been underserved by technology for too long. We're not just helping them keep up. We're helping them achieve results they didn't think were possible. 
We're a small, fast-moving team that has grown aggressively over the past 12 months — and we're just getting started. As an early engineer at Ayra, you won't be maintaining someone else's legacy code or waiting months for your work to ship. You'll be building real systems that run inside real businesses across North America, and you'll feel the impact of your work immediately.
We work with clients from Canada, the US, and beyond. Your career, your skills, and your compensation will grow as the company grows. Our team is like family — we're all here because we genuinely believe in what we're building. We want the best engineers, but equally important, we want the right people. Culture matters to us as much as code. 
As a Full Stack Engineer with a strong backend focus, you'll be working on the core of what makes Ayra tick — backend architecture, third-party integrations, voice AI agents, chat agents, and real automated workflows that perform actions inside our clients' businesses. 
You must be comfortable using AI tools like Cursor and Claude Code to accelerate your work. We are an AI-first team — every engineer here uses AI to move faster and build better.
What You'll Work On:
Backend systems and API integrations with platforms our clients depend on daily
Voice AI agents and chat agents that interact with real customers in real time
Automated workflows that take action inside businesses — scheduling, CRM updates, follow-ups, and more
Our native workflow application and internal tooling
Frontend interfaces built in React / Next.js when needed

job_title: Senior Application Support Engineer – Enterprise Workforce Systems
skills: Microsoft SQL Server | Oracle | Configure | Install | Troubleshoot | IIS | Integration | Application Support | Technical Support | Software | Application | Software Implementation | ASP.NET Core | Middleware | Incident Management | Log Analysis | Workforce Management | Access Control | GCC Projects | Active Directory | Application Deployment & Release Management | SSL | system support | TCP/IP | SQL Server | Zoho CRM
job_description:
Job Description

We are looking for a Senior Application Support Engineer to join a leading Doha-based software company specializing in Attendance, Workforce Management, and Access Control solutions.
The role will handle enterprise deployments and production environments across GCC clients.
This is not a traditional helpdesk role. We are looking for engineers experienced in IIS hosting, SQL Server operations, middleware troubleshooting, and live system support.

Key Responsibilities

You will deploy and maintain enterprise web applications and ASP.NET Core APIs on IIS.
You will troubleshoot complex production issues across middleware, databases, and integrations.
You will manage SQL Server environments, handling performance, jobs, scripts, and data troubleshooting.
You will configure and maintain SSL certificates, HTTPS bindings, and secure deployments.
You will support live client environments through VPN and secure remote access.
You will analyze logs, identify root causes, and collaborate with development teams to implement fixes. You will handle high-priority incidents and ensure system stability for enterprise clients.
job_requirements:
You should have at least 4 years of experience in application support or enterprise technical support.
You need strong hands-on experience with IIS deployment and troubleshooting.
You must be familiar with advanced SQL Server tasks such as queries, jobs, and performance basics.
Experience supporting production environments (not only development) is essential.
A solid understanding of networking fundamentals, including TCP/IP, VPN, firewalls, and SSL is required.
Candidates with only frontend development or basic IT support backgrounds will not be a good fit for this role.

Strong Advantage

Experience with Attendance, HR, Payroll, or Access Control systems will be a significant advantage.
Experience supporting Angular-based enterprise applications is also preferred.
Familiarity with Zoho or other structured ticketing systems will be beneficial.

Why Join

In this role, you will work on large-scale deployments across GCC markets.
You will gain exposure to enterprise clients and complex integrations.
This is a high-impact technical role with excellent growth opportunities.

Job Purpose

Test Engineer is part of one or more scrum teams and works on daily basis to support our change delivery process. Senior Test Engineer delivers ensures that the
